name: Diatam: DTAM
intuition: The clause must contain an overt lexical, phrasal, or clausal adverbial that explicitly anchors the event to a specific past time interval. We are checking for the presence of the adverb, not just the compatibility.
set_to_1_if: The clause contains a lexical past time adverb (e.g., 'yesterday', 'then' [only if sequential], 'recently').
The clause contains a phrasal past time adverb (e.g., 'in 1990', 'last week', 'at 5:00').
The clause contains a clausal past time anchor starting with temporal conjunctions (e.g., 'when', 'after', 'before', 'since' [temporal]).
set_to_0_if: The adverbial is a logical connector rather than a temporal one (e.g., 'What shall we say THEN?' -> Logical inference, not time).
The clause contains conditional/exception markers ('if', 'unless', 'except') that do not establish a specific temporal anchor.
There is no temporal adverbial of any sort present in the text.
edge_cases: Distinguish 'then': If it means 'at that time' (e.g., 'He walked in, then he sat'), it is DTAM (1). If it means 'therefore' (e.g., 'What then?'), it is NOT DTAM (0).
examples_positive: When he arrived, I had left. (Clausal -> 1)
examples_negative: I had not known sin. (No adverb -> 0)
